food_name: Big breakfast (scrambled eggs, English muffin, patty)
aliases: Big breakfast (scrambled eggs, english muffin, patty)

english: Big breakfast (scrambled eggs, english muffin, patty)

serving.natural_language_portion: One plate of big breakfast typically consists of scrambled eggs, an English muffin, and a sausage patty, weighing approximately 250g.

nutrient_source.title: Big breakfast (scrambled eggs, english muffin, patty)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data directly from HPB Singapore for the specified food item. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat are estimated from typical composition of similar Western breakfast items as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish.

gi_source.title: English muffin, white, enriched
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated based on the English muffin component (GI 70), which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor in this composite meal. Scrambled eggs and sausage patty have negligible GI contribution. GL calculated as (70 x 25.77 x 2.5) / 100 = 44.0 for a 250g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (451mg/100g) with insufficient potassium offset (140mg/100g).
hypertension.guidance: This dish is high in sodium (451mg/100g) with a low potassium-to-sodium ratio (140mg vs 451mg per 100g), making it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension; request no added salt, choose unprocessed lean patty, and add potassium-rich sides such as avocado or spinach.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (70) from refined English muffin with low dietary fibre (1.8g/100g) causing rapid glucose absorption.
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a high GI of 70 driven by the refined English muffin and only 1.8g/100g dietary fibre, this dish can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes; substitute the English muffin with a wholegrain alternative and reduce portion size to manage glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (3.5g/100g), estimated trans fat (0.1g/100g), and elevated sodium (451mg/100g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat, estimated trans fat from the processed sausage patty, and high sodium content poses a moderate cardiovascular risk; choose lean poultry or fish as a patty substitute, use olive oil for egg preparation, and limit portion frequency.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat patty provides arachidonic acid and estimated trans fat (0.1g/100g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine production.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The red meat sausage patty contributes arachidonic acid that amplifies TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways, and the estimated trans fat content is pro-inflammatory at any amount; replace with chicken or fish patty and use extra-virgin olive oil for scrambling eggs.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Big Breakfast is a classic Western-style set meal commonly served for breakfast or brunch, consisting of scrambled eggs, a toasted English muffin, and a savory sausage patty. The scrambled eggs are typically light and fluffy, prepared with butter or milk. The English muffin provides a soft, chewy texture with a slightly crisp exterior when toasted. The sausage patty, usually made from seasoned pork or beef, is pan-fried and delivers a rich, umami-forward flavor. Nutritionally, this dish provides 242 kcal per 100g with 11.52g protein, 10.4g fat, and 25.77g carbohydrates. Sodium is notably high at 451mg/100g. The high glycemic index (estimated 70) from the refined English muffin base poses concerns for blood glucose management. The dish is not suitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or rheumatoid arthritis without modification. It carries moderate cardiovascular risk due to saturated fat, estimated trans fat, and sodium content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is directly sourced from HPB Singapore for the exact food item with confidence 0.95. GI is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate component (English muffin, GI 70) using the University of Sydney GI Database as the approved GI source.
notes: Saturated fat (3.5g/100g), monounsaturated fat (4.0g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (2.0g/100g), and trans fat (0.1g/100g) are estimated from typical fatty acid composition of similar Western composite breakfast items (scrambled eggs, English muffin, pork/beef sausage patty) as HPB Singapore does not report fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed micronutrient data for this composite dish is not individually reported in the HPB Singapore food composition database and no equivalent composite entry was found in USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026: vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields require individual component-level analysis or a future composite food laboratory assay to populate accurately.
